title: Open well-known paths for custom domain DNS verification

requirements.text: Configure the React app router to serve the `.well-known` directory publicly without authentication or redirect interference. Ensure that requests to `/.well-known/*` paths (e.g., `/.well-known/ic-domains`) are passed through as static files from `frontend/public/.well-known/` without being intercepted by any route handler, auth guard, or layout wrapper.

quotes: open well-known paths for custom domain

acceptanceCriteria: A GET request to `/.well-known/ic-domains` returns the raw text content of the file without redirecting to a login page or rendering any React component.
The `.well-known` directory is served as purely static content from `frontend/public/.well-known/`.
No authentication check or layout wrapping is applied to `/.well-known/*` paths.
The existing `frontend/public/.well-known/ic-domains` file content (canister principal ID) is preserved and served correctly.

requirements.text: Ensure the TanStack Router configuration in `frontend/src/App.tsx` does not define a catch-all or wildcard route that intercepts `/.well-known/*` before the static file server can handle it. If such a conflict exists, add a route exclusion or a passthrough route for `/.well-known` paths.

quotes: open well-known paths for custom domain

acceptanceCriteria: The TanStack Router does not render any component in response to `/.well-known/*` URL paths.
Static file serving for `/.well-known/ic-domains` is not blocked by any router-level redirect or 404 handler.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/ (immutable paths).
Preserve the existing content of frontend/public/.well-known/ic-domains exactly as-is.
nonGoals: Adding new domain validation UI components.
Changing the canister principal ID stored in ic-domains.
Supporting additional .well-known file types beyond what already exists.
